title: poa bolanderi var. kelloggii
intro: Poa bolanderi var. kelloggii, also known as Kellogg's bluegrass, is a variety of the Poa bolanderi grass species, native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Kellogg's bluegrass include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activities, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Kellogg's bluegrass can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
